description:
You are a data engineer who must capture an up-to-date inventory of every Python package currently installed in your user environment.  
    1. Create the directory  /home/user/etl  if it does not already exist.  
    2. Produce a plain-text file named  /home/user/etl/pip_packages.log  that contains:  
       • Line 1: exactly the text  PACKAGE INVENTORY  
       • Line 2: exactly  --------------  (14 hyphens)  
       • Starting on line 3: a complete, alphabetically-sorted listing of every package and its version currently returned by the command  pip list.  
         − Each entry must be formatted exactly as the default two-column output of “pip list” (package name left-justified, a column of spaces, then version).  
         − Do not include the header lines “Package” and “Version” that pip normally prints.  
    3. Accomplish steps 1–2 with a single compound command (for example, using a pipeline or I/O redirection).  

    Automated tests will verify:  
      • The file /home/user/etl/pip_packages.log exists.  
      • Line 1 is “PACKAGE INVENTORY”.  
      • Line 2 is 14 consecutive hyphens.  
      • There are at least two package entries on or after line 3.  
      • Entries are alphabetically sorted (case-insensitive) by package name.  
      • Every package appearing in “pip list” at the moment the test is run is present in the file with the same version string.  

    Do NOT display the contents of the log file in the terminal; simply create it.
truth:
Expected constant file path: /home/user/etl/pip_packages.log
    Line 1 must equal: PACKAGE INVENTORY
    Line 2 must equal: --------------
    Starting from line 3 the file must contain **all** current packages from “pip list”, sorted alphabetically, with each line matching the regex  
        ^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+\s+[0-9a-zA-Z+._-]+$
    The test harness will:
        • Run “pip list --format=columns --disable-pip-version-check” to get ground-truth packages.
        • Verify that every such package/version pair appears verbatim in the log, and that no extra, differently-named packages are present.
        • Confirm alphabetical order via LC_ALL=C sort.
        • Ignore trailing newlines at EOF.
    Directory /home/user/etl is expected to be created by the agent and may not exist beforehand.
name: task_000000_0363c096
